Robert M. Fomon was a Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co., a firm that was involved in financial services, and he was also a governor of the New York Stock Exchange. While he was not directly associated with the physical location of the New York Stock Exchange at 11 Wall Street, he held significant positions within the exchange's governance structure. Therefore, his connection to the New York Stock Exchange is primarily through his role in its leadership and oversight. 

**Answer:** Robert M. Fomon was a governor of the New York Stock Exchange.
